headline: Claude's expanding tool ecosystem and a wave of retrieval and security techniques dominate the day.
body: Claude moved to the front as tutorials positioned its integrated products (Chat, Code, Cowork, Design, Routines) as an operating system for work and framed Artifacts as a free alternative to paid SaaS. A cluster of retrieval and inference techniques broke out together, including semantic chunking, HNSW, vector search, KV cache compression for long-horizon agents, and kernel fusion. Security techniques surfaced in parallel, with vulnerability detection, guardrails, and prompt-injection defense rising alongside an argument that generative AI has lowered the barrier to cyberattacks and pushed defenders toward an assume-breach posture. Separate talks reframed intelligence as efficiency gained through context and entropy reduction rather than raw compute scaling.
